# arxiv radar

> arxiv radar is a self-hosted arXiv paper discovery platform with semantic search, personalized recommendations, and an MCP server that lets AI agents search and retrieve academic papers.

## MCP Server

The arxiv-radar MCP server provides AI agents with tools to search and retrieve arXiv papers.

- Public URL: https://arxivradar.com/mcp/sse
- Self-hosted URL: http://localhost:8080/mcp/sse
- Transport: SSE (Server-Sent Events)
- Auth: None required

### Tools

- `search_papers(query, limit?, categories?, days?, sort?)`: Semantic search over paper abstracts. Returns titles, authors, abstracts, and links (PDF, HTML, abstract page). Supports category filtering (e.g. "cs.LG,cs.AI"), date range (last N days), and sort order ("relevance", "newest", "oldest").
- `get_paper(paper_id)`: Get metadata and abstract for a single paper by arXiv ID (e.g. "2301.12345").
- `list_recent_papers(limit?, categories?, days?, sort?)`: Browse recently published papers. Defaults to last 7 days, sorted newest first.
- `get_similar_papers(paper_id, limit?)`: Find papers similar to a given paper using embedding vector similarity.

### Paper Output Format

Each paper result includes:
- Title, authors, categories, publication date
- Abstract (full text)
- PDF URL (arxiv.org/pdf/...)
- HTML URL (ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/...) — fetch this for full paper content
- Abstract page URL (arxiv.org/abs/...)

### Connecting

Cursor (.cursor/mcp.json):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "arxiv-radar": {
      "type": "sse",
      "url": "https://arxivradar.com/mcp/sse"
    }
  }
}
```

Claude Code:
```
claude mcp add --transport sse arxiv-radar https://arxivradar.com/mcp/sse
```

Claude Desktop (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "arxiv-radar": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "mcp-remote", "https://arxivradar.com/mcp/sse"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Indexed Categories

The default configuration indexes papers from: cs.CV, cs.LG, cs.CL, cs.AI, cs.NE, cs.RO. This is configurable via the ARXIV_CATEGORIES environment variable.

## REST API

Base URL: https://arxivradar.com/api
OpenAPI docs: https://arxivradar.com/api/docs

Key endpoints:
- GET /api/papers?q=...&days=...&categories=... — search papers
- GET /api/papers/{id} — paper detail
- GET /api/papers/{id}/similar — similar papers
- GET /api/recommendations/for-you — personalized feed (requires auth)
- GET /api/social/trending — trending papers

## Architecture

- Backend: FastAPI (Python, async)
- Database: PostgreSQL 16 + pgvector for vector similarity
- Embeddings: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim, runs on CPU)
- Search: Hybrid semantic + full-text search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
- Frontend: React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Orchestration: Docker Compose (7 containers)
